p.strong: Trap frame rewrite → User handler → Trampoline → sigreturn

g.text: ═══ IRET — Ring 0 → Ring 3 ═══ CPU loads EIP, ESP, EFLAGS from trap frame ═══

pre.code: user_esp = tf->esp; // current user stack saved_eip = tf->eip; // where to resume later saved_esp = tf->esp; // stack to restore later

div.h3: Step 2: Write Trampoline to User Stack

pre.code: // At user_esp, write machine code: B8 1C 00 00 00 // MOV EAX, 28 (SYS_SIGRETURN) CD 30 // INT 0x30 (syscall) EB FE // JMP -2 (infinite loop safety)

div.p: 7 bytes of executable code on the stack

pre.code: push(trampoline_addr); // fake return address push(signum); // handler argument push(saved_esp); // for sigreturn push(saved_eip); // for sigreturn
tf->eip = sa->sa_handler; // jump to handler tf->esp = new_esp; // use modified stack

div.p: When IRET executes → CPU loads these values → Execution redirected to user handler!
Runs in user mode (Ring 3) Full access to user-space functions
Handler's RET instruction pops trampoline address from stack:

pre.code: MOV EAX, 28 ; SYS_SIGRETURN INT 0x30 ; trap into kernel JMP -2 ; safety: never reached

div.p: Kernel reads saved values from user stack:

ul.li: Process continues exactly where it was interrupted
